description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly dark, mottled surface. The stone displays a mix of deep greenish-gray and brown hues, interspersed with lighter patches and a distinct white spot on one side, suggesting mineral inclusions. Its texture is coarse and uneven, with jagged edges and a fractured appearance, indicating it may have broken off from a larger rock. One face of the stone shows a rough, speckled pattern, possibly from weathering or exposure to the elements. The stone's mass and density are apparent from its solid, compact structure, and it appears heavy and difficult to move by hand. Such a stone could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: A rugged, dark mottled stone with jagged edges, mineral inclusions, and a coarse, weathered surface. Features a distinct white patch and a fractured, angular form.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broad, sloped face with mottled greenish-gray coloration and subtle brown undertones.
description_view_1: The stone appears darker and more shadowed, emphasizing its rough, uneven surface and angular top ridge.
description_view_2: A prominent white mineral patch and a speckled, lighter area dominate this side, showing more surface weathering.
description_view_3: This angle reveals the stone's blocky, fractured end and the transition from dark mottling to a lighter, rougher texture.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or roadside verge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Mottled and speckled with irregular patches, including a distinct white mineral spot and lighter weathered areas
attribute_weathering: Rough and uneven with visible signs of surface weathering and minor mineral exposure
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, providing a visual contrast to manicured lawns or paved surfaces
